# UC-22.59.12 · Crew BYOD / passenger Wi-Fi segregation — administrative + crew welfare + passenger network isolation from OT and CBS

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Crew internet and passenger Wi-Fi must never be able to reach the bridge or engine-room computers. We watch the network constantly and ring the alarm if a crew or passenger device tries to peek at the ship's important systems.*

---

## Description

Continuously verifies isolation of crew Wi-Fi, passenger Wi-Fi, and administrative-LAN from CBS / OT zones; raises a notable on any allowed traversal within 1 hour.

## Value

The administrative-to-OT path is the historical entry vector for shore-side ransomware reaching shipboard OT. The Maersk NotPetya impact (2017) is the canonical example. Continuous verification that the boundary is not breached is the most cost-effective ongoing assurance.

## Implementation

(1) Tag every Network_Traffic event with `src_zone` / `dst_zone` based on the firewall zone names. (2) Wi-Fi controller logs (Meraki, Aruba IAP) feed into Network_Traffic via the CIM add-on. (3) Schedule UC-22.59.12 hourly; route any allowed_count > 0 at severity P2 (P1 for OT zones during sea passage).

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.12` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `imo_clause="IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s3-1"`. Every row pins (imo_number, src_zone, dst_zone, packet_count, blocked_count, traversal_indicator).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

A crew member's personal laptop on crew Wi-Fi attempts to scan the IBS subnet. UC-22.59.12 MUST flag traversal_indicator=true within 1 hour.

### Negative scenario

Crew + passenger networks isolated from OT; no traversal observed. UC-22.59.12 MUST record traversal_indicator=false.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.59.4 in production.
- Splunk Network_Traffic CIM-compliant data model on accelerated indexes covering firewall + Wi-Fi controller logs.

## Step 1 — Zone enrichment

Props.conf FIELDALIAS on firewall + Wi-Fi controller sourcetypes to set `src_zone` / `dst_zone`. Maintain a vessel-specific zone-mapping lookup keyed on subnet -> zone.

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.12: IMO Crew/Passenger Wi-Fi Isolation]
cron_schedule = 47 * * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.12,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s3-1
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 3 — Runbooks

**`allowed_count > 0` and `dst_zone in [ibs, ecdis, gmdss, ais, propulsion, dps, cargo-control]` during sea passage** (severity P1): Within 1 hour, isolate the source device via Wi-Fi controller; investigate; document.

**Other allowed-traversal events** (severity P2): Within 4 hours, the Network Architect either updates the policy or remediates.

## Step 4 — Maritime-specific notes

- **Cruise-ship passenger Wi-Fi**: large fleet of devices; scale storage and search performance accordingly.
- **Cargo-vessel crew Wi-Fi only**: small fleet of devices; cheaper to monitor.

## SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count(eval(Network_Traffic.action="allowed")) AS allowed_count, count(eval(Network_Traffic.action="blocked")) AS blocked_count, count AS packet_count from datamodel=Network_Traffic where Network_Traffic.src_zone IN ("crew-wifi","passenger-wifi","administrative") AND Network_Traffic.dst_zone IN ("ibs","ins","gmdss","ais","ecdis","propulsion","cargo-control","engineering") by Network_Traffic.imo_number Network_Traffic.src_zone Network_Traffic.dst_zone
| rename Network_Traffic.* AS *
| eval traversal_indicator = if(allowed_count > 0, "true", "false")
| where packet_count > 0
| sort 0 -allowed_count, -packet_count
| table imo_number src_zone dst_zone packet_count allowed_count blocked_count traversal_indicator
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Crew/passenger -> OT traversal count by vessel. (Panel 2) Blocked vs allowed ratio (the blocked counts should dominate). (Panel 3) Top crew-Wi-Fi devices initiating OT scans. (Panel 4) Per-zone-pair heatmap.

## Known False Positives

1. **One-way time-sync from administrative-LAN to OT NTP**: this is a baseline-approved flow; ensure NTP-only is excluded. 2. **OEM portable diagnostic tool on engineering Wi-Fi**: tunnels through PAM in production; if seen on a vessel without PAM yet, flag and route to PAM-onboarding queue. 3. **Crew TV / IPTV traffic**: should be confined to crew network, but occasionally leaks; tighten the leak diagnosis.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_12_exceptions` with the Network Architect's documented approval.

## References

- [IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 Rev.2 §3.1 — Passenger public networks, administrative + crew welfare](https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/OurWork/Security/Documents/MSC-FAL.1-Circ.3-Rev.2.pdf)
- [BIMCO Guidelines — Crew and passenger Wi-Fi segregation](https://www.bimco.org/about-us-and-our-members/publications/the-guidelines-on-cyber-security-onboard-ships)
- [IACS UR E26 — Cyber resilience of ships, network architecture](https://iacs.org.uk/publications/unified-requirements/ur-e/)
